name: Embalse del Molinar, Villa de Ves — circular
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails around Embalse del Molinar are characterized by the dramatic Júcar River Gorge, featuring imposing rock faces and deep canyons. The region offers a blend of dense pine forests and striking turquoise waters, creating a varied landscape for exploration. Hikers will find escarped terrain with exposed stone masses overlooking the river, providing panoramic views. The area's unique geological formations, shaped by the Júcar River's course, add to the natural appeal. Best family friendly hiking trails around Embalse del Molinar The most popular family friendly hiking route is The cliffs of Tolosa — circular, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 33 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of the Júcar River and surrounding cliffs. Another top favourite among local hikers is Molinar Reservoir loop from Presa del Molinar, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This trail circles the reservoir, providing diverse scenery including water views and forested sections. Local hikers also love the Tunnel exit – Old powder magazine loop from Presa del Molinar, a 5.7 miles (9.1 km) trail leading through historical sites and natural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Embalse del Molinar is defined by the Júcar River Gorge, dense pine forests, and escarped terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Embalse del Molinar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Embalse del Molinar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available around Embalse del Molinar?
A: There are 8 family-friendly hiking trails listed in this guide for the Embalse del Molinar area. These routes offer a range of experiences, from easy strolls to more moderate adventures suitable for families.

Q: Are there any easy circular routes suitable for families with younger children?
A: Yes, for an easier circular option, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087851338/the-cliffs-of-tolosa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The cliffs of Tolosa — circular&lt;/a&gt;. This route is rated easy, spans about 5.3 kilometers, and features manageable elevation changes, making it a great choice for families.

Q: What kind of natural features can we expect to see on these family hikes?
A: The family-friendly hikes around Embalse del Molinar offer stunning natural beauty. You'll traverse dense pine forests, witness the striking turquoise waters of the reservoir and river, and experience the dramatic Júcar River Gorge with its dizzying cliffs and imposing rock faces. The region's escarped terrain provides breathtaking panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical sites or interesting landmarks along the trails?
A: Absolutely! The area is rich in history. You can explore the site of Spain's first hydroelectric power station, established in 1907, and discover a fascinating 4-kilometer disused tunnel nearby. Some routes also pass by the abandoned buildings of La Colonia, a village once home to the dam and power station workers. For a route that includes some of this history, check out &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19412347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunnel exit – Old powder magazine loop from Presa del Molinar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the family trails in Embalse del Molinar?
A: The komoot community highly rates the family-friendly trails in Embalse del Molinar, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Hikers often praise the stunning views of the Júcar River Gorge, the tranquility of the pine forests, and the unique blend of natural beauty with historical industrial landmarks. The variety of routes, from easy to moderate, is also frequently highlighted as a positive aspect.

Q: Are there any longer, moderate circular routes for families with more hiking experience?
A: Yes, for families looking for a longer, more challenging circular hike, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077272945/embalse-del-molinar-villa-de-ves-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Molinar, Villa de Ves — circular&lt;/a&gt; route is an excellent option. It covers nearly 13 kilometers with over 400 meters of elevation gain, offering a rewarding experience with expansive views.

Q: Is there ample parking available near the trailheads?
A: Yes, many of the trails in the Embalse del Molinar region, especially those originating near the dam, feature ample parking, making access convenient for families traveling by car.

Q: Can we find routes that offer great views of the reservoir?
A: Many routes provide spectacular views of the Embalse del Molinar. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38239006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinar Reservoir loop from Presa del Molinar&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase the reservoir, offering panoramic vistas of its turquoise waters and the surrounding escarped landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit Embalse del Molinar for family hiking?
A: While the region can be enjoyed year-round, spring and autumn are generally ideal for family hiking in Embalse del Molinar. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant, with lush vegetation in spring and beautiful colors in autumn. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if visiting during that season.

Q: Are there any routes that connect to nearby towns or villages?
A: Yes, some routes offer connections or pass near charming local areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14658728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá del Júcar – Tolosa Bridge loop from Tolosa&lt;/a&gt; provides an opportunity to explore the surroundings of Tolosa and the historic Alcalá del Júcar area, known for its unique cave houses and castle.
tips: The reservoir was built in 1964 to regulate the flow of the Júcar River and guarantee the supply of water to the population and farmland in the area. It has a storage capacity of about 100 million cubic meters of water. The area is popular for water sports such as fishing, canoeing and windsurfing.
What a beautiful landscape! Enjoy the beautiful views of the river and the cliffs that surround it.

An example of industrial architecture from the beginning of the 20th century and it was in operation until the 1960s. Its main function was the generation of electricity by using the power of the river water. Currently, it is in a state of abandonment.

waypoints.name: View of the River and Canyon Cliffs
